purpose: Quick-reference pitfall table for swarm orchestration. Each row is a distinct mistake (file overlap in waves, missing file paths, modifying initialization.json mid-execution, forgetting to clear progress/, stripping CLAUDE.md conventions, dispatching without user approval, oversize tasks that exhaust worker context, master implementing instead of dispatching, confusing tracker_root vs project_root, skipping the dashboard, working from memory instead of re-reading the command file). Master agents read this before EVERY swarm dispatch as a final sanity check.
gotchas: The 'master agent implementing instead of dispatching' pitfall is bolded as the single worst failure mode — it defeats the entire purpose of Synapse and produces a blind dashboard.
Forgetting to ARCHIVE before clearing progress/ is silent data loss — the rule is archive-first then `rm -f`, never the reverse.
Workers cannot find files you don't specify — never assume the worker knows the project layout, always include full relative paths from project root.
{tracker_root} and {project_root} are different locations and BOTH must be passed in every dispatch prompt — confusing them silently writes code to Synapse or progress files into the project.
If a task touches more than 5 files, decompose further — workers reading 10+ files exhaust context and silently drop work.
Quote CLAUDE.md sections directly in worker prompts; paraphrasing produces non-standard code that needs rework.
After context compaction, upstream task results vanish — downstream prompts must include cached upstream summary, files changed, exports, and deviations.
patterns: Pitfall reference table — three columns (Mistake | Effect | Fix) so the master can scan-and-match against the current dispatch.
Archive-then-clear rule: copy dashboard to Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ then `rm -f progress/*.json` — never clear without archiving.
initialization.json is write-once — all lifecycle data flows through worker progress files.
Plan-then-approval gate before any dispatch — wasted compute if user rejects the approach.
Read the command file (p_track.md / p.md) in full every invocation — do not work from memory.
conventions: Single H2 ('Pitfall Reference') with one markdown table — no per-pitfall subsections.
Mistake | Effect | Fix three-column structure, with the most catastrophic pitfalls (master implementing) bolded inline for emphasis.
